incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and NATO-country military/government officials
incidents.vector: Credential phishing and infrastructure re-use compromised 284 email inboxes, including Ukraine's SAPO, ARMA, the Specialized Prosecutor's Office for Defense, Romanian Air Force, Greek Defense General Staff, and Bulgarian and Serbian officials.
incidents.impact: More than 170 Ukrainian investigator accounts and 67 Romanian Air Force inboxes (including NATO airbases) were accessed between September 2024 and March 2026; stolen emails and operational logs were inadvertently left exposed on attacker infrastructure and discovered by researchers.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into customer-facing systems; details of the initial access vector have not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: Customer reservation details, names, emails, home addresses and phone numbers were exposed. Booking.com says no financial data was taken and has reset reservation PINs for affected customers.

incidents.vector: Two malicious insiders filmed customer data displayed in the exchange's backend; an external group then attempted to extort Kraken using the recordings.
incidents.impact: Kraken refused to pay the ransom, terminated the employees, notified affected customers and law enforcement, and says customer funds were not at risk.

incidents.vector: Data previously stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce tenant during the 2025 extortion wave was leaked on the ShinyHunters dark-web site after Hallmark refused to pay.
incidents.impact: Personal information of roughly 1.7 million Hallmark customers was published online.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into the LA City Attorney's systems; method not yet disclosed publicly.
incidents.impact: Approximately 7.7 TB of data was leaked online, including sensitive case materials, witness names, medical records, internal affairs files, and personal information on Los Angeles Police Department officers.

incidents.vector: Business email compromise against a US subsidiary convinced staff to redirect a legitimate contractor payment to an attacker-controlled account.
incidents.impact: The UK-listed energy company disclosed a loss of approximately €700,000 (about $770,000) from the fraudulent wire transfer.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack disrupted county IT networks and public services; the county was also hit by a separate ransomware incident in January.
incidents.impact: Governor Tim Walz deployed Minnesota National Guard cyber units after the county's IT systems and public services were disrupted.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion at ChipSoft, the vendor behind the HiX electronic patient record system used by approximately 70% of Dutch hospitals.
incidents.impact: ChipSoft confirmed a ransomware attack; the HiX platform reportedly remained available, but the incident underscores concentration risk across most of the Dutch hospital sector.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into NSCC infrastructure; initial access vector has not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: Petabytes of sensitive data, including files marked 'secret' and containing apparent military and government information, were exfiltrated and slowly released publicly starting in March 2026.

incidents.victim: SOHO router owners worldwide and 200+ targeted organizations (Microsoft 365 users, foreign ministries, law enforcement, email providers)
incidents.vector: GRU Unit 26165 exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life TP-Link and MikroTik SOHO routers to modify their DNS settings, redirecting traffic to attacker-controlled resolvers and conducting adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Outlook Web Access to steal OAuth tokens.
incidents.impact: At peak in December 2025 more than 18,000 routers were compromised, enabling credential and token theft from users on 5,000+ devices across 200+ organizations; the FBI's court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' remediated routers across 23 US states on April 7 and Microsoft, NCSC, and DOJ issued coordinated advisories.

incidents.vector: Distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks launched by a pro-Iranian hacktivist group.
incidents.impact: Pinterest was knocked offline for several hours and the website of fintech Chime Financial was also disrupted; the group claimed the attacks publicly.

incidents.victim: US energy and water utilities (3,900+ exposed devices)
incidents.vector: Iranian threat actors are probing and exploiting internet-exposed industrial control devices (PLCs, SCADA) at US energy and water operators, according to federal warnings.
incidents.impact: CISA and partners warned that 3,900+ exposed devices put US energy and water infrastructure in the crosshairs of disruptive Iranian operations; no confirmed outages have been publicly reported.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, professional services, and finance organizations in Australia, UK and US (Medusa ransomware campaign)
incidents.vector: Medusa operators (tracked by Microsoft as Storm-1175) weaponized SmarterMail (CVE-2026-23760) and GoAnywhere MFT (CVE-2025-10035) up to a week before public disclosure, moving from initial access to data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Recent victims include the University of Mississippi Medical Center (Mississippi's largest hospital) and New Jersey's Passaic County; Microsoft flagged the group's ability to exploit zero-days and n-days at 'high velocity' across multiple sectors.

incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack against the firm led to unauthorized access to client data.
incidents.impact: Attackers posted client materials online from one of the largest US corporate law firms (2,400+ lawyers); Jones Day confirmed the breach after leaked data appeared publicly.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into the company's hot wallet infrastructure; specific initial access vector undisclosed.
incidents.impact: Bitcoin ATM operator Bitcoin Depot, which runs 25,000+ machines, disclosed in an SEC filing that approximately $3.6 million in crypto was stolen from its wallets.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3502, a zero-day in the TrueConf video conferencing client's updater, allowing an attacker-controlled on-premises TrueConf server to push malicious updates (Havoc framework, ShadowPad malware) to endpoints.
incidents.impact: A government-operated TrueConf server used by dozens of agencies was weaponized to distribute malware; CISA ordered federal agencies to patch by April 16 and TrueConf released a fix in March. TrueConf serves roughly 100,000 organizations globally.

incidents.vector: Abuse of a stolen AWS API key harvested through the Trivy supply-chain compromise; attackers used the credential to access the Europa.eu platform and related EU cloud resources.
incidents.impact: Approximately 92 GB of compressed data belonging to 42 internal clients and 29 EU entities (including names, email addresses and some email content) was stolen on March 19 and published on ShinyHunters' leak site on March 28. CERT-EU attributed the intrusion to TeamPCP with high confidence.

incidents.vector: Social engineering attack against the company's customer support portal in February 2026.
incidents.impact: Hims & Hers disclosed that customer names, email addresses and other support-ticket data were accessed; the telehealth provider sells weight-loss and sexual-health prescriptions.

incidents.vector: Operatives posing as a quantitative trading firm built trust with Drift staff over months at industry conferences, then delivered a malicious Apple TestFlight app and VS Code extensions that compromised devices belonging to Drift Security Council members, enabling the attackers to multi-sign treasury transactions.
incidents.impact: Drift's protocol treasury was emptied of approximately $280–285 million in crypto assets; investigators tied the operation to a DPRK-aligned group, and Drift has published a post-mortem.

incidents.victim: Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center and Massachusetts towns (Pepperell, Ashby, Dunstable, Groton, Townsend)
incidents.vector: Cyberattack against the shared emergency communications center disrupted linked town and public-safety systems.
incidents.impact: Non-emergency and business phone lines went offline for multiple towns; 9-1-1 calls continued to route, but public-safety dispatching and town IT systems were affected while recovery continues.

incidents.vector: Intrusion forced Hasbro to take systems offline; the company stood up intermediary infrastructure to continue order processing. No group has claimed responsibility.
incidents.impact: Hasbro disclosed the breach in an SEC filing and warned recovery could take several weeks; operational impact to order and shipping systems was acknowledged.

incidents.victim: Axios NPM package, LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy, Cisco and Mercor (TeamPCP supply-chain cascade)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware/extortion (TeamPCP; North Korea suspected in Axios)
incidents.vector: Attackers used stolen developer credentials and social engineering to push malicious versions of widely used open-source packages: Axios (v1.14.1, v0.30.4) pulled in a post-install dependency that deployed OS-specific RATs, while TeamPCP injected credential-harvesting code into LiteLLM, Checkmarx GitHub Actions, and the Trivy scanner, cascading across PyPI, Docker Hub, and VS Code ecosystems.
incidents.impact: Axios (100M weekly downloads) served backdoored releases for roughly three hours; TeamPCP harvested cloud credentials, AWS keys, SSH keys and AI API tokens from thousands of downstream organizations — confirmed victims include the European Commission (92 GB stolen), Mercor (paused by Meta), and Cisco (source code theft and extortion). Google attributes the Axios compromise to a North Korea-nexus actor.
